title: agrostis canina subsp. trinii
intro: Agrostis canina subsp. trinii, also known as Dog's Grass or Red Fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Agrostis canina subsp. trinii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Agrostis canina subsp. trinii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
